subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Prions: The Indestructible Pathogen
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Prions: The Indestructible Pathogen.

hook.text: Prions have no DNA, no RNA, no cell. They're just misfolded proteins. And they're always fatal.

buildup.text: A prion is a normal brain protein that's been folded into the wrong shape.

discovery.text: Misfolded prions convert normal proteins into misfolded copies—a chain reaction that destroys the brain.

twist.text: Prions survive boiling, radiation, bleach, and autoclaving. Standard sterilization doesn't destroy them.

climax.text: Mad cow disease and Creutzfeldt-Jakob disease are prion diseases with no treatment and 100% fatality.

punchline.text: No DNA. No cure. Indestructible. Prions rewrite what a pathogen can be.

quiz.question: What makes prions unique among infectious agents?
options: They are misfolded proteins with no genetic material
